failedAssertions.reason: MIC at block 12500000 hangs indefinitely (>10 minutes) due to cold-start V2 pool loading. On mainnet, ypricemagic must scan ALL ~330k+ Uniswap V2 pairs when looking up an obscure token. With the stablecoin fix removing the USDT=$1 shortcut, MIC's resolution no longer short-circuits early and instead triggers this full pool scan. The implementation fix (extending path to USDC via getAmountsOut) is correct, but the cold-start latency for obscure tokens at historical blocks exceeds the 120s test timeout.
MIC lookup via server API hangs for the same reason as VAL-STABLE-004 — cold-start V2 pool scan at block 12500000. Cannot inspect trade_path until the lookup completes.

appliedUpdates.description: Added cache invalidation notes documenting the three cache layers (sqlite bucket, sqlite price, diskcache) that hold stale $1 values and must be cleared when deploying the stablecoin fix
Added Flow Validator Guidance section for curl-api surface

Bug fix: extend V2 path to USDC for both amount and no-amount cases in get_price(). Previous code called magic.get_price(USDT, historical_block) in the no-amount path, which itself hung after USDT lost stable-usd shortcut.
Bug fix: extend deepest_pool path to USDC for non-USDC stablecoin pairs. Same hang fix for the deepest_pool fallback code path.
Bug fix: return [token_address, paired_with] from get_path_to_stables when paired_with is a non-USDC stablecoin, instead of returning None which triggered full pool scan.

discoveredIssues.description: MIC at block 12500000 hangs for 10+ minutes due to cold-start V2 pool loading on mainnet. All ~330k+ Uniswap V2 pairs must be scanned to find MIC/USDT pool when there's no cache. This happens because (1) factory helper is disabled on mainnet ('Too many pools'), (2) USDT bucket is 'chainlink feed' which causes get_path_to_stables to return None early, and (3) deepest_pool fallback triggers all_pools_for() which awaits __pools__. Fix applied in d75db1b8 (return [MIC, USDT] from get_path_to_stables), but pool loading still blocks until all pairs are fetched from the RPC.
discoveredIssues.suggestedFix: Cache the V2 pool list in SQLite (the pools are already in the Log table, but not in a queryable pool-address form). Alternatively, warm the V2 pool cache at container startup. Or implement a timeout/fallback in get_path_to_stables that gives up after N seconds and returns None rather than hanging.
